item.title: Bureau Of Industry And Security
item.description:
Section 1: Short Title

This Act shall be known as the “Electric Power Industry Reform Act of 2001.”

Maharlikanism Analysis

The EPIRA was enacted to restructure the power sector, ostensibly to lower electricity rates and improve service. However, decades later, the Philippines continues to have some of the highest electricity rates in Asia.

item.title: Constitutional Virtues
item.description:
A New Moral Foundation

Maharlikanism proposes a constitutional revision that embeds virtues as the foundation of governance — not merely rules, but a living moral code rooted in the pre-colonial spirit of Bayani, Bayanihan, and Kagalingan.

item.title: Federalism with Bicameral Parliament
item.description:
The Case for Federalism

The Philippines is one of the most geographically fragmented nations in Southeast Asia — over 7,600 islands, hundreds of languages, and dozens of distinct cultural regions. Yet it is governed as a unitary state from Manila.

item.title: The Fourth Branch of Government
item.description:
The Resources Branch

Maharlikanism proposes a Fourth Branch of Government dedicated to managing Maharlika’s natural and public resources.

The three traditional branches (Executive, Legislative, Judicial) were designed to balance political power. They were never designed to manage economic resources in the public interest.

item.title: The Maharlikan Academy
item.description:
A National Institution for Maharlikan Identity

The Maharlikan Academy is proposed as a constitutional institution — similar to the French Académie française — charged with preserving, studying, and promoting the cultures, languages, and traditions of Maharlika.

item.title: The Philippines 100 Years From Now — Part 4
item.description:
Part 4: The Vision of Maharlika’s Future

Written in 1889 by José Rizal, published in La Solidaridad

With the new men that sprout from their bosom and with the memory of their past, they will work together to strengthen their country, resurrect the maritime and mercantile life natural to them. This and other things may happen within a hundred years or so.
